title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: June 15, 2026: U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Fuels Global Rally

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Spurs Global Market Optimism, Leading to Broad Gains
items.summary: Global markets are rallying on Monday, driven by optimism surrounding a potential peace agreement between the United States and Iran. The deal, which aims to reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz, has led to a significant drop in oil prices and a broad risk-on sentiment among investors. SpaceX's successful Nasdaq debut further amplified the positive mood. While U.S. consumer sentiment showed an upward revision for June, the upcoming Federal Reserve meeting later this week, along with lingering inflation concerns, remain key areas of focus for market participants. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Tech-heavy index buoyed by strong futures.
Nearing record highs amid renewed optimism.

items.headline: SpaceX Soars on Nasdaq Debut
items.summary: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. ($SPCX) saw a spectacular debut on the Nasdaq, surging 19.2% to close at $160.95 on its first day of trading, valuing the company at over $2 trillion.

items.headline: Goldman Sachs Up as Lead Underwriter for SpaceX IPO
items.summary: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. ($GS) rose 2.6% on Friday, benefiting from its role as lead underwriter for the record-breaking SpaceX IPO.

items.headline: Roku Jumps on Acquisition News
items.summary: Roku ($ROKU) shares rose 2.84% in premarket trading following reports that Fox Corp. is acquiring the streaming platform in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $22 billion.

items.headline: Rocket Lab Falls Post-SpaceX IPO
items.summary: Rocket Lab USA, Inc. ($RKLB) experienced a decline of 10.8% on Friday, as investors engaged in profit-taking within the space sector following the massive SpaceX debut.

items.headline: Adobe Shares Dip on CFO Departure
items.summary: Adobe Inc. ($ADBE) saw its shares decline by 6.8% after the company announced the exit of its CFO, Dan Durn.

items.headline: Fox Corp. Declines on Roku Acquisition Announcement
items.summary: Fox Corp. ($FOX) shares fell 9.5% in premarket trading after news broke of its plan to acquire streaming platform Roku for approximately $22 billion.

items.headline: US-Iran Peace Deal Sends Oil Prices Tumbling and Boosts Risk Appetite
items.summary: A significant breakthrough in U.S.-Iran relations, with a preliminary peace agreement announced to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, has dramatically impacted commodity markets. Brent crude fell by 4.9% to $83.74 a barrel, while WTI crude plunged 5.4% to $80.35, easing inflation concerns and lifting global equity markets.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Improves, Fed Meeting Looms
items.summary: The University of Michigan's consumer sentiment for June was revised upwards to 48.9, indicating improving consumer confidence despite lingering inflation concerns. This week, attention shifts to the Federal Reserve's FOMC meeting (June 16-17), where markets anticipate a hold on interest rates, with focus on updated economic projections and Chair Kevin Warsh's press conference.

items.headline: Sector Rotations: Financials and Industrials Lead, Tech Shows Weakening Signs
items.summary: Sector performance indicates a rotation, with Materials, Financials, and Utilities showing strength on Friday. Looking into the current week, Industrials and Health Care are emerging as primary overweights, while Financials are highlighted as the strongest momentum story. Conversely, the Information Technology sector is showing signs of weakening, advising caution on new exposure.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports This Week
items.summary: The week of June 15, 2026, features earnings reports from several notable companies. Among the most anticipated are Kroger ($KR), Progressive ($PGR), Accenture ($ACN), Jabil ($JBL), CarMax ($KMX), and Dave & Busters Entertainment ($PLAY). Powerfleet ($POWW) already reported strong Q4 and full-year fiscal 2026 results, with revenue up 11% year-over-year.
